failure_modes.text: Teams underperform when they lock onto the first plausible idea and never generate enough variation to discover better options.
failure_modes.source_quote: Most useful when the current answer set is too narrow, path-dependent, or biased by the first idea that seemed plausible.
failure_modes.mitigation: Generate before converging: require a minimum number of distinct options before allowing evaluation, so the first plausible idea does not become the only one considered.

failure_modes.text: Experimentation becomes waste when teams celebrate variation but never define the rule for selection, pruning, and learning.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when teams celebrate variation without defining the selection rule.
failure_modes.mitigation: Define selection criteria upfront: before running experiments, specify what evidence would cause an option to be killed, kept, or iterated — variation without selection rules is waste.

failure_modes.text: People can preserve weak answers by processing evidence to protect an early commitment instead of letting tests eliminate bad variants.
failure_modes.source_quote: Commitment and consistency bias can hinder the Variation phase by discouraging deviation from initial choices.
failure_modes.mitigation: Protect honest elimination: assign evidence evaluation to someone other than the option's champion so that confirmation bias cannot preserve weak variants.

premortem_questions.text: What promising option would never appear if we stopped at the first workable answer?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Quantity often drives quality in idea generation.

premortem_questions.text: What is the exact evidence rule by which we will eliminate weak variants instead of admiring them indefinitely?
premortem_questions.source_quote: The Selection phase follows, applying rigorous, evidence-based criteria.

premortem_questions.text: Which assumption collapses our favorite option if we actually test it today?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What is the simplest, most vital hypothesis we can test today that could collapse the entire solution if proven wrong?

heuristics.text: Widen the option set first, then prune with explicit evidence criteria.
heuristics.source_quote: The essence of this model lies in the duality of divergence and convergence.

heuristics.text: Treat solutions as experiments when the future is uncertain and the strongest option is not obvious ex ante.
Do not confuse more options with more learning unless the selection rule is visible and enforced.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger when teams celebrate variation without defining the selection rule.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the chunks close to divergence-plus-selection discipline, evidence-based pruning, and anti-commitment safeguards.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader innovation-process sprawl beyond the clearest generate-and-test moves.
open_questions: The source supports multiple experimentation frameworks, but the Wave 2 surface stays on disciplined variation and pruning.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on experimentation, MECE, and outside-view discipline.
antagonists: The source is strongest against commitment-and-consistency-bias, confirmation-bias, and under-variation from familiar-tool lock-in.
structured_tensions: The clearest tensions are with status-quo-bias, commitment-and-consistency-bias, and confirmation-bias.
